title: List timeline endpoint with replies_policy
description:
## What
Implement GET /api/v1/timelines/list/:id returning a paginated timeline of posts from accounts in the specified list, with replies_policy filtering applied.

## Why
The list timeline is the primary value of the feature — operators and users create lists to get a focused reading experience.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social /api/v1/timelines/list/:id to capture the exact JSON response shape and Link header format; test with replies_policy values 'list', 'followed', 'none' on mastodon.social to observe which reply posts are included or excluded in each mode; confirm that since_id/max_id/min_id/limit query params work the same as home timeline.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: implement GET /api/v1/timelines/list/:id; verify list belongs to authed persona (404 otherwise); query posts WHERE account_id IN (SELECT account_id FROM list_accounts WHERE list_id = ?) with pagination (since_id/max_id/min_id/limit, default limit 20, max 40); apply replies_policy: for 'none' add AND in_reply_to_id IS NULL; for 'list' add AND (in_reply_to_id IS NULL OR in_reply_to_account_id IN (SELECT account_id FROM list_accounts WHERE list_id = ?)); for 'followed' add AND (in_reply_to_id IS NULL OR in_reply_to_account_id IN (SELECT target_id FROM follows WHERE source_id = ?)); exclude posts from muted accounts; serialize as Status objects; set Link header with next/prev.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: create list with two accounts, post from each, assert both appear in list timeline; post a reply from account A to account B, test all three replies_policy values and assert correct inclusion/exclusion; test pagination with since_id/max_id.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social /api/v1/timelines/list/:id live fixture for response shape; mastodon.social observed replies_policy behavior for each of the three enum values.
- Test approach: seed DB with known posts in a list, execute timeline query for each replies_policy value, assert exact set of post ids returned matches expected set derived from mastodon.social-observed behavior.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- List ownership verified before returning any posts — prevents cross-persona timeline reads
- replies_policy must be read from DB at query time, not cached in memory, so PUT updates take effect immediately
- Muted accounts are excluded regardless of replies_policy
- Pagination uses snowflake IDs; since_id/max_id/min_id comparisons must use integer comparison (not string sort) on the stored INTEGER id
- limit capped at 40; default 20; never trust client-supplied limit without bounds check
